title: Scandinavians cycle to work even on snowy days, here’s why that makes sense

paragraphs.english: Across Scandinavia many people commute to work or school on their bikes during winter, despite snow and freezing temperatures.
paragraphs.pidgin: Across Scandinavia plenti pipul commute to work or school on dem bikes during winter, despite snow and freezing temperatures.

paragraphs.english: For example, in the Stockholm region of Sweden around 34% of people cycle at some point during the winter months. Oslo in Norway has seen an increase of winter cycling recently. With 15 % of all journeys being made using this form of transport in winter in 2022, up from 7% in 2015.
paragraphs.pidgin: For example, for di Stockholm region of Sweden around 34% of pipul cycle for some point during di winter months. Oslo for Norway get see an increase of winter cycling recently. Wit 15 % of all journeys being make using dis form of transport for winter for 2022, up from 7% for 2015.

paragraphs.english: I live in the small town of Lund in southern Sweden and I am a winter cyclist. Even in temperatures as low as -5°C to -10°C, with snow on the ground, I cycle from home to take one child to school, and another to preschool and then to work on my bike. It’s around 5km every weekday. For me that is the best and cheapest option. Most of my route is on bike paths through the city. In winter I usually wear a warm jacket, but nothing particularly special for cycling. Most other parents bring their kids to school by bike, even in winter.
paragraphs.pidgin: I live for di small town of Lund for southern Sweden and I dey a winter cyclist. Even for temperatures as low as -5°C to -10°C, wit snow on di ground, I cycle from house to take one pikin to school, and another to preschool and then to work on my bike. E’s around 5km every weekday. For me dat na di best and cheapest option. Most of my route na on bike paths through di city. For winter I usually wear a warm jacket, but notin particularly special for cycling. Most other parents bring dem kids to school by bike, even for winter.

paragraphs.english: And cycling in winter is not just a trend in Sweden. Research shows that the Danish capital, Copenhagen, also has high cycling levels in the colder months. Around 66% of Copenhagen’s cyclists say they use their bikes in the winter. Cyclists there report feeling often prioritised and safe, and many see cycling as an efficient mode of transport. Even in winter, cycling often remains the fastest and most convenient option for many urban journeys.
paragraphs.pidgin: And cycling for winter na no just a trend for Sweden. Research shows dat di Danish capital, Copenhagen, also get high cycling levels for di colder months. Around 66% of Copenhagen’s cyclists tok dem use dem bikes for di winter. Cyclists there report feeling often prioritised and safe, and plenti see cycling as an efficient mode of transport. Even for winter, cycling often remains di fastest and most convenient option for plenti urban journeys.

paragraphs.english: In Sweden, especially in the northern part of the country, studded winter tyres for bikes are fairly common, although where I live in Lund they tend not to be used.
paragraphs.pidgin: For Sweden, especially for di northern part of di country, studded winter tyres for bikes dey fairly common, even though where I live for Lund dem tend no to be use.

paragraphs.english: Studies show that studded bicycle tyres significantly improve bicycle grip and braking on ice.
paragraphs.pidgin: Studies show dat studded bicycle tyres significantly improve bicycle grip and braking on ice.

paragraphs.english: Cycle tracks that are covered with snow can thaw and then freeze creating slushy icy paths that are particularly difficult to manage. Many Scandinavian cities, such as Copenhagen, address this challenge by making sure winter maintenance for cycling routes is given a high priority. Timely snow clearance and anti‑slip treatments, such as combining gravel with salt for better grip, improve safety and encourage people to continue cycling even in cold weather.
paragraphs.pidgin: Cycle tracks dat dey covered wit snow fit thaw and then freeze creating slushy icy paths dat dey particularly difficult to manage. Plenti Scandinavian cities, such as Copenhagen, address dis wahala by making sure winter maintenance for cycling routes na give a high priority. Timely snow clearance and anti‑slip treatments, such as combining gravel wit salt for better grip, improve safety and encourage pipul to continue cycling even for cold weather.

paragraphs.english: Studies show that surface conditions strongly affect cycling decisions.
paragraphs.pidgin: Studies show dat surface conditions strongly affect cycling decisions.

paragraphs.english: Snow clearance remains the most important factor in people’s decisions about whether to get on their bikes, while anti‑slip measures help reduce crashes. Good city maintenance of cycle tracks and appropriate equipment encourages people to continue to use this form of transport throughout the winter.
paragraphs.pidgin: Snow clearance remains di most important factor for pipul’s decisions about whether to get on dem bikes, while anti‑slip measures help reduce crashes. Good city maintenance of cycle tracks and appropriate equipment encourages pipul to continue to use dis form of transport throughout di winter.

paragraphs.english: Advice from local authorities often emphasises safe winter cycling practices, such as adjusting speed to cope with winter conditions. It’s these factors, along with knowledge of how to cycle in bad weather and good path clearance that help cyclists get to work even in demanding weather.
paragraphs.pidgin: Advice from local authorities often emphasises safe winter cycling practices, such as adjusting speed to cope wit winter conditions. E’s dis ones factors, along wit knowledge of how to cycle for bad weather and good path clearance dat help cyclists get to work even for demanding weather.

paragraphs.english: Also significant is that cities in Sweden and Denmark usually offer tracks separated from the roads, and give cyclists clear priority at junctions with cars. Features such as cycling bridges and specific traffic signals for cyclists make the journey easier and minimise conflict with motor vehicles, which becomes especially important when visibility is low or surfaces are wet.
paragraphs.pidgin: Also big na dat cities for Sweden and Denmark usually offer tracks separated from di roads, and give cyclists clear priority for junctions wit cars. Features such as cycling bridges and specific traffic signals for cyclists make di journey easier and minimise wahala wit motor vehicles, which becomes especially important when visibility na low or surfaces dey wet.

paragraphs.english: In places where infrastructure is not as well suited for cycling or mixed with fast-moving traffic, winter conditions exacerbate stress and discourage cycling.
paragraphs.pidgin: For places where infrastructure na no as well suited for cycling or mixed wit fast-moving traffic, winter conditions exacerbate stress and discourage cycling.

paragraphs.english: Winter cycling has significant implications for sustainable transport policies and reaching climate goals. Each cyclist represents one fewer trip with a car having to start in cold weather, a type of journey associated with disproportionately high emissions and local air pollution. Studies suggest that improving winter maintenance levels of cycle paths could reduce accidents and winter cycling more generally reduces car use.
paragraphs.pidgin: Winter cycling get big implications for sustainable transport policies and reaching climate goals. Each cyclist represents one fewer trip wit a car having to start for cold weather, a type of journey associated wit disproportionately high emissions and local air pollution. Studies suggest dat improving winter maintenance levels of cycle paths fit reduce accidents and winter cycling more generally reduces car use.

paragraphs.english: Sustained winter cycling also helps reduce congestion and eases pressure on public transport during peak periods.
paragraphs.pidgin: Sustained winter cycling also helps reduce congestion and eases pressure on public transport during peak periods.

paragraphs.english: Strong links between public transport and cycling also help people tackle longer journeys. Good cycle parking facilities at stations or being able to take the bike on a train also encourages cycling for parts of a journey.
paragraphs.pidgin: Strong links between public transport and cycling also help pipul tackle longer journeys. Good cycle parking facilities for stations or being able to take di bike on a train also encourages cycling for parts of a journey.

paragraphs.english: Winter cycling in Scandinavia is not because people are unusually hardy but because of consistent planning choices, reliable maintenance and a cultural commitment to cycling as an everyday activity. When cities treat cycling as essential transport rather than a seasonal option, people continue to cycle, even in the snow.
paragraphs.pidgin: Winter cycling for Scandinavia na no because pipul dey unusually hardy but because of consistent planning choices, reliable maintenance and a cultural commitment to cycling as an everyday activity. When cities treat cycling as essential transport rather than a seasonal option, pipul continue to cycle, even for di snow.
